# jjezs_reslate BBAAR

## Character

Operator-handbook sweep applying BBAAN's settled cult-verb vocabulary (and any producer-output discipline changes) to the SA-management surfaces in `Tools/rbk/rbh0/`. Lands after BBAAN so narrative describes settled vocabulary, not intent. Designed to run parallel to coding paces — operator-facing prose surface doesn't block depot/foundry work.

Different cognitive posture from coding paces it reflects — prose with intentional ceremony tone. Best held in a separate session from the coding officium.

## Scope

Five handbook files in `Tools/rbk/rbh0/`: `rbhogw_governor_wrapper.sh`, `rbhocr_credential_retriever.sh`, `rbhocd_credential_director.sh`, `rbhopw_payor_wrapper.sh`, `rbhob_base.sh`. Sweep cult-verb constant references and producer-output narrative to BBAAN's settled vocabulary.

## Operator-visible behaviors from BBAAN

These behaviors must surface in narrative; the handbook is the operator's contract.

**Divest removes the production RBRA.** After successful cloud DELETE, the role's local production RBRA file is also removed. Operator narrative should state this explicitly so the disappearing file isn't a surprise.

**Invest is fatal if the SA already exists.** Re-keying flow is divest-then-invest. Narrative should guide the operator to divest first when re-investing.

**Unmake silently absorbs governor cleanup.** No separate governor forfeit step in the procedure. Operator behavior is unchanged ("just run unmake"); the absence-of-mantle case is invisible to the operator (404-tolerant under the hood).

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
- Handbook narrative describes what landed in BBAAN — read the actual code state, do not work from intent. If implementation differs from paddock-settled vocabulary, raise it; do not silently reconcile.
- Preserve the existing operator-narrative tone — ceremony language is intentional. Verb swaps should keep the same cadence (e.g., the parenthetical `(Charter):` pattern stays as `(Invest):`, not flattened into prose).
- Use existing `buh_line` / `buh_step` / `buh_section` / `buh_tt` primitives — no narrative-structure rework.
- Real-time review at each step before commit. No pre-engineered choices.

## Out of scope

- Code logic changes beyond what's required to consume renamed RBZ/RBYC constants.
- Handbook files outside the five listed — depot-identity narrative is already swept by BBAAM Step 1; non-SA handbooks (crucible, windows, tadmor, first-crucible, dispatch CLI) don't touch the changing surfaces.
- Spec/README/RBYC vocabulary documentation — owned by sibling pace BBAAQ (already wrapped).
- Restructuring handbook section ordering or step decomposition — out of scope for a vocabulary sweep.
- New onboarding content — out of scope.

# jjezs_reslate BBAAH

## Character

Final integration pace. Wires pristine-pass as a precondition for `/rbk-prep-release` so the upstream contribution ceremony cannot proceed without a recent pristine green. Closes the loop: pristine qualification becomes load-bearing on release prep, not optional ceremony. Adds documentation for the post-success cleanup ceremony operators need given the gauntlet design omits automatic teardown.

## Work

Modify `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` to add a precondition step: verify rbw-tP ran green against the current HEAD. If not, prep-release fails-fast with operator-visible message naming the marshal-zero → pristine sequence.

Recency contract is **exact-HEAD-match** — strongest invariant, prevents stale passes. rbw-tP green tally writes an artifact linking pass to HEAD's commit SHA; `/rbk-prep-release` reads it and refuses on mismatch. Artifact mechanism (file location, contents, gitignore status) is a mount-time decision.

Update README "Release Procedure" section to reflect that the prep-release step now enforces rbw-tP had to pass against the very HEAD that prep-release is preparing.

Document the post-success cleanup ceremony — since the gauntlet suite omits teardown so operators can peek at final state, document the manual cleanup sequence (`rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU`) operators run when ready to release the canonical infrastructure. README and/or RELEASE.md is the placement decision at mount time (coordinate with BBAAI).

## Verification

- `/rbk-prep-release` fails fast when no pristine pass is recorded for current HEAD
- After successful rbw-tP, `/rbk-prep-release` proceeds normally
- After successful rbw-tP followed by an unrelated commit, `/rbk-prep-release` refuses (SHA mismatch)
- Pristine-passed artifact's gitignore status is correct (deliberately not committed)
- Documentation accurately describes the precondition and the post-success cleanup ceremony

## Out of scope

- Restructuring the rest of `/rbk-prep-release` (preserved as-is)
- Multi-pristine-run aggregation
- CI integration
- Recency policies beyond exact-HEAD-match (other policies left for future heats if needed)

# jjezs_reslate BBAAg

## Character

Final assembly of the gauntlet test suite. Defines the suite as a composition of fixtures, creates the suite tabtarget, and pivots the rbw-tP workbench tabtarget from the originally-planned orchestrator-script shape to entry-contract enforcer + suite tabtarget exec. No new test cases; this pace is composition + entry point only.

Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time.

## Scope

- Gauntlet suite definition: bash array in the engine holding the ordered fixture sequence (per paddock's "State ladder, fixtures along it")
- Resolver branch in the suite-name resolver
- New TestSuite tabtarget for the gauntlet suite
- Reshape rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh: enforce entry contract (marshal-zero signature, RBRR blank, RBRA absent, regimes blank) then exec the gauntlet suite tabtarget

## Cross-pace dependencies

- gauntlet-canonical-onboarding-sequence landed (final new fixture exists)

## Out of scope

- First-run end-to-end validation (BBAAX)
- Pristine-passed sentinel artifact (BBAAH)
- Documentation updates beyond what the workbench tabtarget needs (BBAAI)

content.text:
Three edits landed.

## Pace order check

Current order:

```
₢BBAAR  handbook (parallel-safe, no deps)
₢BBAAd  gauntlet-engine-foundation
₢BBAAe  gauntlet-canonical-establish    needs d
₢BBAAc  pristine-walkthrough-manual     needs e
₢BBAAf  gauntlet-canonical-onboarding-sequence  needs e + c
₢BBAAg  gauntlet-suite-assembly         needs f
₢BBAAX  rbw-tP-first-run                needs g
₢BBAAH  phase-6-prep-release-integration needs X (something to gate on)
₢BBAAI  release-doc-revision            needs heat to settle
```

Dependency-wise: correct. The chain BBAAd → e → c → f → g → X → H → I is dep-respecting. BBAAR sits at the front because it's parallel-safe (no deps).

**One judgment call worth surfacing**: BBAAR being first means `jjx_orient` will recommend it as next-actionable. If you intend to mount the coding chain first (BBAAd) and do BBAAR in a separate session later, you might prefer BBAAR last so orient points at BBAAd. If you'll do BBAAR soon (or in parallel right away), keep it first. Either is dep-correct; this is just about which pace orient's pointer suggests.

If you want me to move BBAAR to the end, say the word — `jjx_relocate BBAAR last` is a one-liner.

## Other things to do before clearing

Honestly, I think we're in good shape. Quick checklist:
